title: crepinia surculosa
intro: Crepinia surculosa, also known as the climbing crepinia or wild yam, is a perennial plant species in the family Asparagaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The plant has been associated with various health benefits, including reducing pain, fever, and inflammation, as well as supporting the immune system. Crepinia surculosa can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being.
